visualization.title: Scholarship counts by type for each semester type

resultRaw.sql: SELECT s.semester_type AS semester_type, s.scholarship_type AS scholarship_type, COUNT(*) AS scholarship_count FROM public.scholarships s GROUP BY s.semester_type, s.scholarship_type ORDER BY s.semester_type ASC, s.scholarship_type ASC;

issues: Visualization does not specify an explicit domain/order for semester_type — if you need a specific semester ordering (e.g., Fall, Spring, Summer, Winter), provide that ordering so the x-axis sorts as intended.

The current chart uses grouped bars via xOffset (one bar per scholarship_type within each semester). If you intended stacked bars (cumulative counts per semester), change the encoding to use stacking instead of xOffset.
Metadata sample shows only a subset of rows (rowCount=6 but sample contains 2 rows) — this is not an error but indicates the sample is truncated and may not show all categories.

visualization.title: Scholarship counts by type for each semester type

result.sql: SELECT s.semester_type AS semester_type, s.scholarship_type AS scholarship_type, COUNT(*) AS scholarship_count FROM public.scholarships s GROUP BY s.semester_type, s.scholarship_type ORDER BY s.semester_type ASC, s.scholarship_type ASC;

issues: Visualization does not specify an explicit domain/order for semester_type — if you need a specific semester ordering (e.g., Fall, Spring, Summer, Winter), provide that ordering so the x-axis sorts as intended.

The current chart uses grouped bars via xOffset (one bar per scholarship_type within each semester). If you intended stacked bars (cumulative counts per semester), change the encoding to use stacking instead of xOffset.
Metadata sample shows only a subset of rows (rowCount=6 but sample contains 2 rows) — this is not an error but indicates the sample is truncated and may not show all categories.
